title_syndicate_footer: The Syndicate is watching. The Protocol is running.
create_select_bg: Pick your background (not a class — that comes later):
bg_office_worker_d: Six years of corporate. The printer is afraid of you. +1 INT.
bg_mechanic_d: You know every engine sound. Your hands are already calloused. +1 INT, +1 STR.
bg_nurse_d: You have seen worse. +1 WIS, +1 CHA.
bg_cook_d: Your kitchen was hell. The dungeon has lower standards. +1 CON, +1 DEX.
bg_security_guard_d: Your job: stand and wait. You trained for this. +1 STR, +1 CON.
bg_courier_d: Three blocks, two seconds, one floor. +2 DEX.
bg_student_d: Sleep-deprived, overthinking, hardened by boredom. +2 INT.
bg_streamer_d: You know how to be watched. The dungeon likes that. +2 CHA.
bg_soldier_d: You've been elsewhere already. This pays less. +1 STR, +1 CON.
bg_unemployed_hustler_d: Nobody's looking for you. Nobody remembers you. +1 CHA, +1 DEX.
bg_janitor_d: You know every dead end. +1 CON, +1 INT.
bg_paramedic_d: Calm in situations where others scream. +1 WIS, +1 INT.
log_first_room_hint: (Hint: try 'look', 'search', 'break' or 'strip' something — scraps let you 'craft' an item. Type 'help' for more. Mass commands: 'rozbierz wszystko' (strip all), 'weź wszystko' (take all).)
feedback_mass_salvage_intro: You start a methodical dismantle pass.
feedback_mass_salvage_nothing: Nothing here looks worth taking apart.
feedback_mass_salvage_safehouse_warn: Some of these belong to the safehouse — there will be consequences.
feedback_mass_search_intro: You comb through everything that looks worth attention.
feedback_mass_loot_intro: You take everything you can carry.
feedback_mass_loot_safehouse_warn: Some of these belong to the safehouse — someone is watching.
feedback_mass_break_intro: You take a deep breath. This is going to be loud.
feedback_mass_break_summary: The noise carries farther than you planned.
feedback_combat_defend: You brace. The next hit lands softer.
feedback_combat_out_of_range: '{name}' is out of melee range. Close in or use something at range.
feedback_combat_assessed_already: You already know what you can without a closer look.
feedback_combat_no_exit: No way out you can see.
feedback_combat_flee_fail: You can't break off — the hostiles seize the moment.
feedback_combat_no_trap: No armed trap to lure them into.
feedback_combat_env_prone: The environment turns on '{name}' — knocked down.
log_class_offer: The Syndicate has a class proposal for you.
log_at_exit_locked: You stand at the floor exit. Still locked.
feedback_no_intent: I don't understand what you want to do.
feedback_no_target: You don't see what you're looking for here.
feedback_silence: You hear only your own breath.
time_deadline_missed: Deadline passed. The dungeon seals the floor.
class_bruiser_d: You hit hard and first. That works more often than it should.
class_survivor_d: Specialty: not dying. Everything else is a bonus.
class_saboteur_d: The room will kill itself if you help it.
class_engineer_d: Every machine near you becomes either useful or illegal.
class_ranger_d: You know where to wait. And what to shoot with.
class_medic_d: You keep others alive. Sometimes for your own reasons.
class_occultist_d: The dungeon whispers. You listen. Everyone regrets it.
class_negotiator_d: Your words are cheaper than bullets and just as effective.
class_trickster_d: Master of small betrayals and large plans built on them.
class_demolitionist_d: If it exists, it can be blown up. Or collapsed.
class_showman_d: The audience pays. Sponsors bid up.
class_scout_d: You see first. You come back whole. Sometimes.
narrator_class_offer_1: The Syndicate reviewed your footage and has suggestions.
narrator_env_kill_1: The dungeon is filing a complaint against you with legal.
narrator_impossible_action_1: That would look great on screen if it were possible.
narrator_salvage_success_1: You take it apart with the calm of someone past sentiment.
narrator_salvage_success_2: The world lost structure. Your bag gained volume.
narrator_salvage_partial_1: Half of it worked. The other half stayed somewhere along the way.
narrator_salvage_fail_1: Something snaps the wrong way. Material stays. Dignity, mostly.
narrator_salvage_critical_fail_1: Something flicks into your eye. Inventory will wait for the hospital arc.
narrator_furniture_salvage_1: The chair objects. The chair is overruled.
narrator_tech_salvage_1: The panel yields. The schematic resolves, a little late for the designer.
narrator_bathroom_salvage_1: It used to be plumbing. Now it's material. Hygiene was the trade.
narrator_safehouse_theft_attempt_1: They notice. They don't react now. They react slowly, like every bad landlord.
narrator_safehouse_theft_escalation_1: Second time. Third won't be a warning.
narrator_sponsor_property_salvage_1: Sponsor gets a notification. Sponsor dislikes notifications.
narrator_corpse_harvest_1: You cut where you can. The bag gets heavier. So does the rest.
narrator_monster_harvest_1: What's left was what the creature didn't need for active duty.
narrator_disgusting_but_useful_1: The material checks out. No one asks where, unless you tell them.
narrator_everything_is_material_1: In this dungeon, everything is part of something. Some of it becomes yours.
narrator_craft_success_1: It works. Don't ask why. Asking lowers the odds.
narrator_craft_partial_1: Something got built. Something also broke. Net positive.
narrator_craft_fail_1: The material pushes back. So does the trade. Time slips.
narrator_craft_critical_fail_1: Something popped in the middle of it. Material gone. Hand almost.
narrator_absurd_craft_attempt_1: What you're trying to build isn't in any known training program.
narrator_unstable_item_created_1: It came out, but it vibrates. Keep it away from your pack. And your head.
narrator_improvised_trap_created_1: Trap assembled. Waiting for someone having a worse day than yours.
narrator_improvised_weapon_created_1: A close-range argument, ready. Provisionally convincing.
narrator_improvised_tool_created_1: A small thing that adds one die. That's enough.
narrator_deploy_trap_1: Trap is set. The dungeon now has a second layer of intent.
narrator_deploy_trap_success_1: Placed. Quiet. Wait. The rest is just time.
narrator_deploy_trap_fail_1: The mechanism won't latch. You leave it for next time.
narrator_trap_self_trigger_1: That was the fastest demo the audience has seen all day.
narrator_rare_material_found_1: Something the dungeon shops don't list. Yours, for now.
narrator_sponsor_component_found_1: A part with the sponsor's logo. Hard to say if that's good news.
narrator_anomalous_material_found_1: Material doesn't fit any known category. Science claims they don't care.
narrator_forbidden_material_harvested_1: You took something you weren't supposed to. No one stopped you. Someone logged it.
narrator_audience_likes_recycling_1: The audience likes recycling. The sponsor pretends not to.
narrator_audience_disgusted_1: The audience turns away. Some for show. Some for real.
narrator_sponsor_files_complaint_1: Sponsor legal opens a new folder. It already has your highlight color.
narrator_belief_seed_attempt_1: System logged an attempt at semantic violence.
narrator_belief_seed_success_1: The idea found a crack to fit through. The rest is a matter of time.
narrator_belief_seed_partial_1: Some bought it. Some rewrote it. The version that escapes won't be yours.
narrator_belief_seed_fail_1: Words bounced off the walls. The room acknowledged and did nothing.
narrator_belief_seed_backlash_1: Something is coming back at you. It has your name on it and looks worse.
narrator_belief_spreads_1: The idea repeats. Repetition warps it. Warping repeats too.
narrator_belief_distorts_1: Your thesis returns from the far side of the room with three errors and more confidence.
narrator_machine_confusion_1: The machines fall silent for a beat. That isn't an error. That's worse.
narrator_crawler_gossip_shift_1: The safehouse conversation pivots. You didn't push — you left the push here earlier.
narrator_sponsor_notices_propaganda_1: The sponsor expresses surprise. Surprise already has a timestamp and a case number.
narrator_absurd_idea_takes_root_1: It shouldn't have worked. It did. The origin will need a separate question.
feedback_memetic_no_claim: No content — what exactly are you trying to convince them of?
feedback_memetic_no_target: Nobody here to hear or read this.
feedback_memetic_no_channel: You have no way to push this through the '{channel}' channel here.
feedback_memetic_success: Idea planted. Now to see whose head it lodges in longest.
feedback_memetic_partial: Some of it sticks, in a version you didn't plan.
feedback_memetic_fail: No one took it. A trace remains, but only a trace.
feedback_memetic_backlash: The argument turned on you. Someone's repeating it now, but about you.
feedback_belief_spreads: The rumor you seeded finds new mouths.
feedback_belief_distorts: Your idea changed owners — and meaning.
feedback_belief_burned: One of your ideas burned out and left silence behind.
ui_beliefs_empty: Ideas: nothing of yours is circulating yet.
ui_mode_text_hint: Type freely · empty input + arrows = actions · Enter = pick · Esc = clear
ui_mode_nav_hint: Up/Dn select   Left/Right/Tab group   Enter confirm   Esc text
controls_help_1: Text mode: type in Polish. Enter submits. Up/Down for history.
controls_help_2: Select mode: [T] enter, Up/Down select, Left/Right/Tab group, Enter confirm.
controls_help_3: Hotkeys: I inventory, M map, C character, J knowledge, R rest,
controls_help_4: Ctrl+S save, F1 help, Esc text mode / clear.
ui_resolution_hint: Commands: fullscreen / windowed / set resolution WxH
journal_footer: Left/Right or Tab: tab  ·  Up/Down: select  ·  PageUp/Dn: list  ·  Shift+PageUp/Dn: detail  ·  Esc: close
journal_help_3: Up/Down: select entry   PageUp/PageDown: scroll list
journal_help_4: Shift+PageUp/PageDown: scroll details   Enter: reset detail scroll
settings_footer: Up/Down: select   Left/Right: change   Enter: apply   Esc: back
feedback_clue_feels_off: It sounds consistent, but something doesn't fit. Worth treating with care.
feedback_password_unknown: You don't know any password that fits here.
feedback_password_no_target: You have a password, but nothing here matches it.
feedback_weakness_no_target: No one here to use a known weakness on.
feedback_weakness_unknown: You know something hurts them. You don't know what.
feedback_weakness_used: You strike where it hurts. '{name}': -{amount} HP.
feedback_weakness_missed: You know where to hit. You miss.
feedback_invoke_no_seed: No idea you know about would land on them.
feedback_invoke_no_match: That myth hasn't reached this room yet.
feedback_invoke_no_channel: The drones hear words. They have no reason to treat them as orders.
feedback_invoke_no_target: No one here this could apply to.
feedback_invoke_ok: You remind '{name}' of what's circulating about them. The target hesitates.
feedback_invoke_miss: The idea didn't catch at the right moment.
narrator_clue_path_used_1: Information you picked up earlier just earned its place in your bag.
narrator_clue_path_used_2: Knowledge turns into action. That moment is rarer than it sounds.
narrator_false_lead_followed_1: The lead was bad from the start. Some crawlers were too.
narrator_rumor_revealed_useful_1: What you heard at the counter only now fits the rest of the picture.
narrator_machine_talks_back_1: A machine in the corner finishes a sentence no one started. It fits the rumor.
narrator_rumor_echo_1: Someone in the room mutters something suspiciously familiar. Your line got here first.
narrator_belief_backfires_1: Your idea comes back from the other side of the room — in a version you didn't sign.
narrator_sponsor_mocks_belief_1: The sponsor folds your idea into the briefing with a '/sarcasm' tag.
narrator_crawler_mentions_distorted_beli: A crawler near you repeats your words off by one. That one word flips the meaning.
narrator_target_hesitates_1: The target reaches for a decision and sets it down again.
narrator_target_reinterprets_order_1: Someone read that order their own way. Good for you. Bad for whoever wrote it.
narrator_target_demands_proof_1: They're asking how you know. That means they've already heard.
help_1: Commands: look, inspect X, search, listen <exit>,
help_2: go to <room>, use X, attack X, talk to X,
help_3: push X into Y, hide, flee, rest, wait,
help_4: inventory, materials, clues, beliefs, character, map, save, help,
help_5: strip X, dismantle X, harvest bones from X,
help_6: craft trap / knife / smoke bottle / bandage,
help_7: deploy trap, place tripwire, plant smoke bottle,
help_8: craft help, salvage help, trap help
narrator_audience_rise_1: Ratings tick up. The top-bar flickers brighter.
narrator_audience_rise_2: The audience leans in. It's not applause yet, but it's something.
narrator_audience_rise_3: The channel registers a spike. The sponsor takes a note.
narrator_audience_drop_1: The audience drifts. Someone switches to the weather channel.
narrator_audience_drop_2: Interest fades. The sponsor checks their watch.
narrator_audience_drop_3: Dead air. Ratings sink and the sponsor sinks with them.
sponsor_novachem_tagline: It works. It hurts. It sells.
sponsor_novachem_heckle_1: NovaChem: 'Subject 7B is showing promising durability. Continue.'
sponsor_novachem_heckle_2: NovaChem: 'Please do not expire outside of lab hours.'
sponsor_novachem_heckle_3: NovaChem: 'The chest is twitching in a useful way.'
sponsor_sport_heckle_1: Sport Sponsor: 'No points for dodging. Lack noted.'
sponsor_sport_heckle_2: Sport Sponsor: 'Class B- combat. We require Class B before the segment ends.'
sponsor_sport_heckle_3: Sport Sponsor: 'Blood: yes. Dust: yes. Scream: barely audible.'
sponsor_czarny_rynek_tagline: Everything has a price. Two prices.
sponsor_czarny_rynek_heckle_1: Black Market: 'Brother, you have left a lovely mess. I have a gift for you.'
sponsor_czarny_rynek_heckle_2: Black Market: 'That lock opens politely too. You do not have to, but you could.'
sponsor_czarny_rynek_heckle_3: Black Market: 'The value of what you broke just dropped. The value of what I sell — up.'
sponsor_ministerstwo_heckle_1: Ministry: 'Remember: we were always right. Always.'
sponsor_ministerstwo_heckle_2: Ministry: 'This never happened. Say it out loud.'
sponsor_ministerstwo_heckle_3: Ministry: 'Your dossier is current. Good that you asked.'
sponsor_recykling_heckle_1: Recycling Cult: 'Everything returns. The table returns. The bot returns. You return too, brother.'
sponsor_recykling_heckle_2: Recycling Cult: 'You waste. Wasting is a sin against metal.'
sponsor_recykling_heckle_3: Recycling Cult: 'Reassemble. But differently. But the same. You understand.'
sponsor_kanal7_heckle_1: Channel 7: 'And folks — one hit point left… WE WILL SEE THIS AFTER THE ADS.'
sponsor_kanal7_heckle_2: Channel 7: 'We have a hero of the week. Or a corpse of the week. We will find out.'
sponsor_kanal7_heckle_3: Channel 7: 'This is the Edge. This is where it HAPPENS. This is where we MAKE MONEY.'
bg_opiekun_zwierzaka_d: You get along with animals better than with people. You start with one random pet.
companion_feedback_none_owned: You don't have a companion with you right now.
companion_feed_no_food: You have nothing to feed the pet. Find a snack bar or rations.
companion_calm_success: You speak softly. The pet breathes slower. (stress -3, bond +1)
companion_calm_partial: The pet isn't fully convinced but stops thrashing. (stress -1)
companion_scout_implausible: This pet isn't built for scouting. It looks at you with reproach.
companion_scout_lost: Your pet vanishes around a corner. It doesn't come back.
companion_scout_success: Your pet comes back with something. It can sense things.
companion_scout_fail: Your pet comes back with nothing useful.
companion_lure_combat: Your companion makes a scene. Your next attack has advantage. (stress +3)
companion_lure_exploration: Your pet puts on a show — nobody's looking at you for a beat. (stress +3)
encounter_alarm_default: An alarm howls down the corridor. Patrol incoming. ~30 minutes.
encounter_alarm_silent: A silent alarm fires somewhere. You wouldn't know. ~15 minutes.
encounter_alarm_biotech: Biohazard containment protocol. NovaChem is dispatching an inspector. ~45 minutes.
encounter_warn_t15: ~15 min until patrol arrives. Time to act.
encounter_warn_t1: Right around the corner. You can hear footsteps.
encounter_fired_hidden: You hear their boots. They search. They don't find you. After a while, they leave.
encounter_fired_traps_only: Your traps did the work. Nobody else is walking in here.
encounter_fired_player_fled: Distant voices in the room you abandoned. Patrol took it.
encounter_prep_time_close: Patrol is already at the door.
dot_burning_tick: You're on fire. {amount} HP to ashes.
dot_bleeding_tick: You bleed. {amount} HP outside the body.
status_burning_extinguished: {name} smolders but goes out — they were wet.
status_chilled_burned_off: Ice meets fire. Steam. Both statuses cleared.
ui_monitor_hint: Switch: 'monitor N' (takes effect on next launch).
ui_monitor_set: Monitor: {idx}. Takes effect on next launch.
